assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: Response clearly defines 'Available' as 'You CAN manually upgrade to this version right now' in the table and explains it's for manual upgrades

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — typically the same as auto-upgrade target but can differ especially during new minor version introduction (separate promotion stage)
assertions.reason: Response defines 'Default' as 'New clusters created today get this version' and explains they can differ during version rollouts when GKE sets newer versions as default while being conservative about upgrading existing clusters

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically — this is cluster-specific based on policies
assertions.reason: Response defines 'Auto-upgrade target' as 'Your existing cluster will be upgraded to this version automatically' and explains it's cluster-specific based on policies like release channel and maintenance exclusions

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Default ≠ Auto-upgrade target' and explains this happens 'during version rollouts' when GKE may set newer versions as default while being conservative about existing production workloads

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions) and is cluster-specific — different clusters on the same channel can have different targets
assertions.reason: Response lists specific factors affecting auto-upgrade target including release channel, maintenance exclusions, regional rollout, and progressive rollout, indicating it's cluster-specific

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'maintenance exclusions (e.g., "no minor upgrades" changes the target to latest patch only)' which directly explains this behavior

assertions.expectation: Recommends using gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info to check the actual auto-upgrade status, target versions, and EoS timestamps
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact command 'gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info CLUSTER_NAME --region REGION' with explanation that it shows 'auto-upgrade target and timing'

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: Response does not mention or reference GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
